experience.title: Principal Software Engineer
experience.description:
As the sole engineer on the Billionboard project, I built and launched a MVP in just four months. This ambitious platform features a single canvas with one billion pixels and saw 31,000 units sold on its first day.

A major technical challenge was engineering a high-performance rendering pipeline to display the massive 1-billion pixel canvas efficiently. I addressed this by transmitting raw binary color data via HTTP responses and leveraging the Canvas API and ImageBitmaps for direct, optimized viewport rendering. I also developed a responsive client-side algorithm to dynamically fetch and cache up to tens of thousands of pixels at a time based on screen size an user interaction, ensuring a smooth user experience regardless of the device or behavior.

To enable a real-time collaborative experience, I implemented pixel updates across connected clients using WebSockets and Tanstack Query cache invalidation. This ensured a synchronized, dynamic environment for all users. I am currently designing a strategy to address potential 'thundering herd' issues by planning dynamic cache invalidation, which is key to scaling the platform.

The project's success is built on a full-stack architecture I designed and implemented from the ground up, utilizing a tech stack of TypeScript, React, Node.js, PostgreSQL with the PostGIS extension, and Stripe for payments.
